detailedExemplarData.title: To what extent did Greta Gerwigâs past representation of cultural femininity shape her portrayal of Barbie?

subject.title: English B
subject.description:
**IB English B** is a **Group 2** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **language acquisition through five prescribed themes: identities, experiences, human ingenuity, social organization, and sharing the planet**, with a strong focus on **developing receptive, productive, and interactive communication skills in English as a second language**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2025**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Paper 1** | Productive skills - writing | 1 hour | 1 hour 15 minutes | 25% |
| **Paper 2** | Receptive skills - listening and reading comprehension | 1 hour 45 minutes | 2 hours | 50% |
| **Individual Oral** | Internal assessment - oral communication | 15 min prep + 10 min exam | 15 min prep + 10 min exam | 25% |

strengths: Topic is communicated accurately and effectively with clear and appropriate purpose and focus.
Research question is clearly stated, focused and effectively connected to the discussion.
weaknesses: Methodology is only partially articulated, with no dedicated section explaining source selection and evaluation.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Two of the three strands (topic communication and research question) demonstrate performance at the top 5â6 band, while methodology falls in the 3â4 band. Averaging these performances yields a final score of 5, reflecting strong focus and question clarity tempered by an incomplete methodological account.

strengths: Application of source material is clearly relevant and advances the argument throughout.
Subject knowledge is clear and coherent, with effective use of a wide variety of scholarly and primary sources.
weaknesses: Use of terminology is mostly accurate but occasionally imprecise or inconsistently defined (e.g., coinages like ârepresentativityâ).
Some descriptive narrative (historical overview) momentarily displaces deeper conceptual exploration.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Source application and subject knowledge both align with the top band (5â6), while use of terminology and occasional descriptive lapses place performance at the upper 3â4 band. A balanced assessment warrants a score of 5.

strengths: Research is good, drawing on a diverse and credible bibliography consistently tied to the research question.
Analysis and discussion construct a coherent, reasoned argument that returns regularly to the RQ and synthesises multiple lines of evidence.
weaknesses: Depth of analysis is uneven, with sections that drift into descriptive summary rather than critical evaluation.
Limited engagement with contrasting theoretical frameworks and few explicit counter-arguments restrict the incisiveness of the evaluation.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All three strands lie solidly within the 7â9 band: research quality is clear, analysis has minor inconsistencies, and discussion is reasoned though not fully exhaustive. A score of 7 reflects strong critical thinking with room for deeper theoretical engagement and more consistent analytical depth.

strengths: The essay follows the prescribed EE structure with clear section headings and a functional table of contents.
weaknesses: Page numbers are missing and citation formatting is inconsistent across parenthetical and footnote styles.
Layout issues (inconsistent indentation in the TOC, unformatted block quotations, bibliography spacing) impede professional presentation.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While the basic structural requirements are met, significant formatting and layout omissions place performance at the lower end of the 1â2 band. A score of 2 reflects presence of structure but multiple presentation flaws.

strandSelections.reasoning:
The research questionââTo what extent did Greta Gerwigâs past representation of cultural femininity shape her portrayal of Barbie?ââis explicitly stated on the title page, in the introduction and at the start of subsequent chapters, demonstrating consistent prominence.  Its scope (examining three prior films plus wider scholarly commentary) is suitably focused for the word limit and allows for sustained, relevant discussion.

Throughout the essay the student returns to the RQ when analysing Gerwigâs filmography, script excerpts and critical reception, showing a strong logical thread.  While a few contextual detours are somewhat lengthy, they ultimately funnel back to the guiding question.  Overall, the RQ is clear, sharply delimited and drives the analysis, aligning with the highest band (5â6).

Analytical sections link historical developments, script excerpts and critical commentary to argue how Gerwigâs earlier portrayals inform Barbieâs feminist framing.  Comparative readings of dialogue (e.g., Kenâs patriarchy monologue) alongside empirical data on car safety or consumerism illustrate a multi-layered approach.

However, depth is uneven: some chapters drift into descriptive plot summary or catalogue Barbieâs product history without fully integrating these details into the central evaluative thread.  Logical reasoning predominates but occasional leaps in inference and limited counter-analysis temper overall incisiveness.  This places the work at the lower end of the 7â9 band for analysis depth.

Primary evidence (screenplay extracts, direct dialogue, box-office data) and a wide variety of scholarly and journalistic sources are woven directly into the argument.  Citations are well integrated to illuminate points about Barbieâs cultural evolution, feminist critique and Gerwigâs auteur traits.  The student does not merely insert quotations but analyses their significance, demonstrating clear relevance to the RQ.

Although occasional quotations could have been paraphrased, and source evaluation is sometimes cursory, the overall application of evidence consistently advances the discussion rather than decorating it.  These qualities satisfy the descriptor of "clearly relevant and appropriate application," meriting the top (5â6) band.

Key layout conventions (title, word count declaration, spacing) are met, but page numbersâa mandatory elementâare absent, and indentation inconsistencies in the table of contents impede readability.  Citation style fluctuates between parenthetical and footnote formats, signalling uneven application of style guidelines.

Because several layout elements are present yet others are incorrect or missing, the work satisfies only the lower descriptor of the 1â2 band for layout considerations.  A score of 1 within this band is appropriate given the frequency and impact of the issues identified.

The essay demonstrates solid knowledge of Barbieâs cultural history, Gerwigâs filmmaking background and feminist film theory.  Concepts such as patriarchy, mumblecore, satire and consumerism are explained accurately and used to underpin analysis.

There is, however, occasional over-reliance on descriptive historical narrative (e.g., lengthy chronology of doll development) that momentarily displaces deeper conceptual exploration.  These lapses prevent fully âexcellentâ coherence but still reflect clear, well-structured subject knowledge with effective support from sources, warranting the lower half of the 5â6 band.

The essay follows the prescribed EE structure (title page, abstract omitted as per new guide, introduction, body, conclusion, bibliography) and employs clear section headings that guide the reader logically through the investigation.

Nevertheless, mechanical issuesâmisaligned table of contents, absence of page numbers, and some unformatted block quotationsâindicate that elements of the structure are missing or improperly executed.  These shortcomings correspond to the 1â2 band, with performance toward the upper end because the basic structural framework is present.

The bibliography lists 20+ diverse and largely credible sources (peer-reviewed journals, historical texts, interviews, screenplay).  Each is tied back to the research question, and primary evidence is balanced with scholarly critique.  The breadth and pertinence of material reflect good investigative diligence.

Minor gaps remain: there is limited interrogation of popular-press reliability and few contrasting scholarly viewpoints.  These shortcomings preclude âexcellentâ (10â12) performance but the research still demonstrates clear relevance and thoughtful selection, aligning with the 7â9 band at a solid level.

The student constructs a largely coherent argument that returns to the research question in sections and in the conclusion, weighing supportive and oppositional critical views (e.g., accusations of âanti-manâ bias versus feminist acclaim).  Evaluation of Gerwigâs narrative strategies (role inversions, satire) shows insight and moves beyond mere description.

Yet the discussion occasionally settles for asserting significance rather than demonstrating it, and the concluding judgment, while clear, could be better supported by explicit synthesis of earlier evidence.  Given these minor inconsistencies, the strand aligns with âgoodâ discussion and evaluation in the 7â9 band, meriting a score of 7.

The student briefly references compiling primary (screenplay, film scenes, interviews) and secondary (academic articles, news pieces) sources, and the selection is largely appropriate.  However, there is no dedicated methodology section explaining how or why particular sources were chosen, nor any explicit criteria for evaluating reliability or limitations.

Some critical comments on bias in popular-press materials and notes on academic credibility appear piecemeal within chapters, which offers partial evidence of informed selection.  Nonetheless, the lack of a systematic account of research methods and source evaluation means methodology cannot be considered fully âcompleteâ.  These characteristics correspond most closely to the middle band (3â4), with performance leaning toward the lower end of that range.

Key theoretical terms are generally accurate: patriarchy, matriarchy, consumerism, American Dream, etc.  The student also introduces discipline-specific ideas like âGerwig-ian styleâ and âmumblecoreâ correctly.

Nevertheless, imprecise coinages such as ârepresentativityâ and inconsistent citation formatting undermine linguistic precision.  A few concepts (e.g., âcultural villainâ) are asserted without clear scholarly definition.  Overall terminology use is âmostly accurateâ but not consistently rigorous, matching the 3â4 band at its upper point.

annotations.comment: The declared word count (4000) at the start of the report satisfies the formatting requirement and confirms adherence to the maximum limit.
Indentation and leader dots in the table of contents are inconsistent; realign entries and apply uniform spacing for professional presentation.
The table of contents is provided with section titles and corresponding page numbers, guiding readers through the essayâs structure.
The introduction provides a clear and coherent definition of âcultural femininity,â establishing a solid conceptual foundation for the investigation.
The Guinness World Records citation is missing specific page or entry numbers; ensure all references include precise bibliographic details.
There is no critical appraisal of Barbie Media (2022); evaluate potential promotional bias or limitations to strengthen source selection rationale.
The connection between globalization, American cinema, and Barbieâs international spread effectively ties source material to the research question.
Citation formatting fluctuates between parenthetical and footnote styles; choose one consistent format and apply it uniformly throughout.
The work omits an abstract; include a concise 300-word abstract summarizing the research question, methodology, and key conclusions to meet EE structure requirements.
The analysis of consumerism and beauty standards is well supported by secondary sources, deepening the discussion of cultural femininity.
The bibliography demonstrates extensive research with diverse source types, reflecting breadth and relevance.
Bibliography entries are formatted inconsistently (varying italics, numbering); adopt a single citation style (e.g., MLA or APA) and apply uniformly.
URLs and text links appear in multiple styles; standardise hyperlink presentation and remove embedded HTML for clarity.
Bibliography lacks hanging indents and uniform line spacing; adjust layout to improve readability and meet presentation conventions.
Recognition of Gerwigâs realistic dialogue and cinematography techniques demonstrates insightful linkage to her earlier works.
Discussion of power dynamics would benefit from engagement with a theoretical framework (e.g., Foucault on power relations) to deepen analysis.
The chronological overview of Barbieâs evolution from 1959 onward provides valuable context for later comparative analysis.
Several sentences exceed recommended length, hindering clarity; break complex constructions into shorter, focused statements.
The overview of Gerwigâs signature styleâcombining feminine melancholy and witty humorâcorrectly frames her auteur traits for analysis.
The term âGerwig-ianâ is coinage without academic definition; provide a sourced explanation or clarify its derivation to ensure scholarly rigour.

The studentâs title clearly communicates the investigationâs focus and purpose, effectively framing Greta Gerwigâs portrayal of Barbie within cultural femininity and situating the study in English B context.
The discussion of cinemaâs teaching role, while interesting, drifts from the research question; refocus this paragraph to directly link film as cultural text to your RQ.
There is no dedicated methodology section explaining how sources were selected or evaluated; include a clear methods subsection to outline criteria and research procedures.
Integration of quotations from Alfonsi and Richard-Cox shows the studentâs ability to weave historical perspectives into the argument with relevance.
Although the TOC lists page numbers, actual pages lack footer pagination; add page numbers on each page to facilitate navigation.
This section reads as descriptive plot summary rather than analysis; streamline background details and tie each point back to how Gerwigâs past work informs Barbie.
Results from the Anschutz & Engels study are presented without interpretation; explain how findings on doll size and food intake support your argument on cultural femininity.
Reference to Lim (2007) on Mumblecore lacks direct quotation or summary; embed a clear definition from the original source to support your argument.
The sentence describing cinematography ends abruptly; complete the thought and explicitly connect how filming style shapes Barbieâs portrayal.
The analysis of Barbielandâs inversions is incisive, directly relating filmic satire to the research question on cultural femininity.
The Gerwig quotation lacks a page reference; include the precise page number for all primary source citations per style guidelines.
Interdisciplinary data on crash injury disparities enriches the argument and shows effective use of empirical evidence.
The statistic on â73% more likelyâ injury lacks methodological context; specify study parameters and sample size in methodology discussion.
HTML underline tags appear in the body text; replace these with standard formatting (italic or bold) to maintain professional style.
Inclusion of film criticsâ reactions broadens the evaluation and demonstrates engagement with public discourse around Barbie.
The shift to criticsâ analysis is abrupt; add a transition sentence explaining why reception analysis is methodologically relevant.
Block quotations exceed three lines without indentation; reformat long quotes as separate blocks per citation style guidelines.
The Sasha quote effectively balances the argument by acknowledging longstanding public criticism of Barbieâs impact.
Analysis does not explicitly link Sashaâs critique back to Gerwigâs earlier films; draw clearer connections to support your RQ.
Quotes spanning multiple lines lack consistent citation formatting; ensure page references are clearly indicated and styled appropriately.
The conclusion adeptly synthesizes the inversion theme with the research question, demonstrating coherent argument closure.
The final sentence is truncated mid-thought; complete the statement to clearly articulate the studyâs overall implications.
